medical_history.chief_complaint: acute cholecystitis in the setting of atrial fibrillation with RVR
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ lady with a-fib on Coumadin, and diastolic HF, who is admitted to the MICU with acute cholecystitis in the setting of rapid atrial fibrillation. She initially presented to an OSH (___) on ___ due to elevated INR of 12 on labs drawn at home, and upon arrival was found to have abdominal pain, RR 22, and O2 sat of 95% on RA in the setting of an INR of 9.8, WBC 13.1, and T Bili 2.2. Abd CT revealed cholelithiasis with external biliary duct dilatation (unchanged from CT on ___. HIDA scan was positive for acute cholecystitis. She was in atrial fibrillation with RVR, and due to her age/comorbidities, she was considered to not be a surgical candidate so she was transferred to ___ today for ERCP and percutaneous cholecystostomy.
medical_history.past_medical_history: TIAs (transient speech deficit), Afib, diastolic heart failure, EF reportedly 65%, dyspnea on exertion at baseline, pacemaker placement, DM2 controlled with diet, severe constipation, mild dementia, knee replacement x3, appendectomy.
medical_history.family_history: Nobody with DM2, liver disease, or MI. No family history of any malignancies.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: T: 96.2 BP: 97/51 P: 100 R: 18 O2: 92% 4L NC. General: Alert, oriented to person/city/date/year, no acute distress. Abdomen: obese but nondistended, (+)BS, tender to palpation of medial RUQ; negative ___ sign; no rebound tenderness or guarding, no organomegaly. Ext: extremely edematous (2+ throughout); warm, well perfused, 2+ DP pulses.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Crackles at bilateral bases. CV: S1 and S2, irregularly irregular, SEM heard at LSB.
